Some medical field which offe best work and life balance are shown below -
Dentistry (BDS & MDS) – Fixed clinic hours, fewer emergencies.
Pharmacy (BPharm & MPharm) – Less stress compared to hospital-based jobs.

Public Health & Community Medicine – Focus on preventive healthcare rather than emergency treatments.
Clinical Research & Medical Writing – Research-based work with no direct patient responsibilities.

review_summary.college_name: Santhiram College Of Pharmacy Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: Doctor of Pharmacy (Pharm.D.) - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Review of Santhiram College in Shiksha.

content.Placements: My batch is in the Pharma.D course, which is a 5-year course with a 1-year internship. It is a course similar to the MBBS course but based on pharmaceuticals. The highest package is up to 5 LPA, while the lowest is 2 LPA. The recruitment companies are mostly pharmaceutical companies. The roles offered include assistant doctor, pharmacist, and drug inspector.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure in our college is good, and I love it the most. Facilities are good, and the hostel facilities are the best among other colleges. However, sports are disappointing in our college. The mess food is native and super, with no problems. Labs, classrooms, and library are all A-grade.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2021
individual_reviews.title: Review of Santhiram College Of Pharmacy.

content.Placements: About 100 students in our course are placed. The lowest salary package is about 2.5 LPA. The highest salary package is about 7.5 LPA. Top recruiters are Apollo, Reddy's Laboratories, and Santhiram. About 85% of students got internships in our course in those companies. Top roles offered in our course are drug inspector and product manager.
content.Infrastructure: Lab facilities, chemicals, and instruments in the labs are good. The infrastructure is normal and neat. The quality of the hostel is good. The quality and taste of the food are good. The mess and canteen are good.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2019
individual_reviews.title: This college worthful for local students.

content.Placements: Around 80% of students were placed on campus. The highest salary package offered was 2.5 LPA. Dr. Reddy's, Hetero, Divis were the top recruiting companies. Internships were provided by this college, but they encourage to get internships from the companies. R&D is the top role offered.
content.Infrastructure: In this college, classrooms were very spacious. Wi-Fi was not available. The library was available but updated journals were not available. Study textbooks were available, and the quality of the food was good. Medical facilities were available at all times. Sports were conducted by this college.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2019
individual_reviews.title: It is not worthy and needs improvement in all aspects especially in the practical part.

content.Placements: Only a few students got placed. Top companies like Hetero, Divis, Omega Health Care visit the campus but they did not provide any internships. No top roles were offered. Even the salary packages offered were also not up to the mark. Many students will not join the roles offered by the placement cell.
content.Infrastructure: There was no proper equipment in the laboratory and its maintenance was very poor. There was no updated software available. No Wi-Fi was provided and the computers were not working properly. The medical facilities were good. There were no games and sports training and guidance. The hostel was good and the food served was tasty.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2021
individual_reviews.title: I'm absolutely satisfied because of faculty members, good placements and really good infrastructure.

content.Placements: About 80% of the students were placed from this course. The highest salary offered is more than Rs. 80,000 per month, and the lowest salary offered is below Rs. 30,000 per month. The average salary offered is Rs. 50,000 per month. Recruiting companies here are Divi's Labs and many pharma companies. Students get internships in Santhiram Hospitals. Around 70% to 90% of the students get internships. Roles offered in this course are pharmacist, drug inspector, etc.
content.Infrastructure: In our college, there is a huge library with many books. There is a separate lab for pharmaceutics, chemistry pharmacology, pharmacognosy, etc. There are many classrooms with separate sections. The hostel is also available near the college, and the quality of the food served is good. There are many facilities and a canteen. Sports and games are also available.

reviews_list.review_title: Love hate relationship with my college.

review_content.Placements: There are no internships or requirements held at our college. Only 1-2 students selected and got an opportunity of being a staff at our college. There is nothing other than that. We have our regular internship which is of 1 year. It is mandatory by KSDC.

reviews_list.review_title: The college is located in a remote area.

review_content.Placements: No placement after course completion. As a student, you are posted in different locations. No stipend is paid to postgraduates. Only the intern is paid at college. The stipend is very low. Exposure is very limited.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is good. Sports facility is poor. Hostel food quality is average. Generator facilities are available in the department.
review_content.Faculty: HOD of the department is very good. He takes a lot of effort to make the concepts clear. He is very knowledgeable. He teaches the theory with clarity and gives a free hand to learn in the operation theatre.

individual_reviews.title: It's a good college but needs improvement.

content.Placements: About 60% to 80% of students were placed. The highest salary package was 3 LPA, and the lowest was 2 LPA. Many companies offered jobs. Around 60% of students got internships from our course.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi in the hostel was average since many students used it. Labs were clean but some instruments needed improvements. Classrooms were good but the walls were not good. The library was also good but could be improved.
content.Faculty: Teachers were helpful during the exams. They provided important questions and cleared doubts. They were qualified and knowledgeable. The teaching quality was 3.5 out of 5. The pass percentage was 40%. Exams were hard for students who didn't study.

review_content.Placements: 65 percent of students got placement in my college last year. The highest package is around 15LPA and the average package is around 6LPA, and the lowest package is around 3.6LPA. They got opportunities from Big companies. Top roles offered were HRs and Accountants. Etc.
review_content.Infrastructure: Lack of Wi-Fi, not good infrastructure, the whole college old buildings need renovation, lack of smart class, teachers have to teach with traditional methods of writing on the board, etc. New Education Policy Books are not available in the library for many courses like ours. The college has a mess and canteen facility, but lacks medical facilities and many sports to play on campus.

review_summary.college_name: LNMU Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Computer Applications(BCA) - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: A budget friendly BCA college that focuses more on academics than placements.

content.Placements: In my batch, placements were average because CM College mainly focused on academics rather than corporate hiring. Around 10 to 15 percent of students got placed, mostly through off campus opportunities. Many students, including myself, chose to pursue higher studies like MCA or other masters programs after completing the course, because placement participation was limited.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure of CM College was basic but functional. Classrooms were spacious and well ventilated. Though some needed modernization. Computer labs were available with sufficient systems for BCA students. Advanced software and the latest hardware needed improvement.

individual_reviews.course: B.A. in Political Science - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: I am absolutely satisfied with this college during my session 2022–2025.

content.Placements: 30% of students are placed in my course. The highest package is 12 LPA, and the lowest is 1 LPA offered in this course. Random companies recruit for this course. 60% of students get internships through our course. Most companies are related to professors, teachers, etc. in this course at my college.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi and labs are not available at my college, but classrooms are available. There are many classrooms in my college. All classrooms are open for students. The library is also available in my college for reading books and self-study.

individual_reviews.course: B.Com. (Hons.) in Financial Accountancy - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Overall experience of my college life.

content.Placements: 65 percent of students got placement in my college last year. The highest package is around 15LPA and the average package is around 6LPA, and the lowest package is around 3.6LPA. They got opportunities from Big companies. Top roles offered were HRs and Accountants. Etc.
content.Infrastructure: Lack of Wi-Fi, not good infrastructure, the whole college old buildings need renovation, lack of smart class, teachers have to teach with traditional methods of writing on the board, etc. New Education Policy Books are not available in the library for many courses like ours. The college has a mess and canteen facility, but lacks medical facilities and many sports to play on campus.

individual_reviews.course: B.A. in History - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Satisfied with the college as per the fees.

content.Placements: In this college, there are no placement criteria for the students, and no students get placed in this college. They do not provide any type of training for interviews, internships or placement. No companies have come to give interviews to the students, and no placements have been seen.
content.Infrastructure: Talking about their infrastructure overall, it was good that they provided a good classroom to their students, but they did not provide any type of canteen or medical facilities. But they provide sports games, and no library, Wi-Fi or labs are available for the students.

individual_reviews.course: B.Sc. in Chemistry - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: APSM College, Barauni is an affiliated unit of Lalit Narayan Mithila University, Darbhanga.

content.Infrastructure: It is a nice college. The college emphasizes activities like sports, NCC, and NSS for the all-round development of the students. The campus is equipped with Wi-Fi. It has a library, language laboratory, and various counselling cell (career counselling, grievance, women empowerment, etc). It is located in Barauni, Begusarai district, Bihar. But there is no hostel facility.
content.Faculty: It is affiliated with Lalit Narayan Mithila University, Darbhanga. Faculty members help students in their learning and exam preparation. The council is run by various departments, and organized by seminars and conferences. All teachers are amazing, very supportive, and understand all the students in every academic area. APSM College follows stringent academic standards to provide quality education.

admission_overview.description: LNMU admissions are both merit and entrance-based and the applications can be completed either online or offline. The university admits students based on both merit and entrance exam scores, depending on the programme. Lalit Narayan Mithila University offers a wide range of courses at the undergraduate, postgraduate, and diploma levels. These programmes are offered in fields, such as Arts, Management, Commerce, Science, Computer Applications, and more. The university accepts national-level exams, such as CAT/ MAT/ NEET, etc. in addition to conducting its Common Entrance Test (CET). eligibility_selection.title: LNMU Eligibility & Selection Criteria 2025
eligibility_selection.description: Lalit Narayan Mithila University admission 2025 is both merit and entrance-based. LNMU offers an extensive range of courses across a wide range of specialisations in the fields of Engineering, Science, Law, Arts, Education, and Medicine. All these courses are offered via the eight faculties, 43 constituent colleges, and 25 affiliated colleges under Lalit Narayan Mithila University. In addition to these, it also has two government colleges, namely Darbhanga Medical College & Hospital and Rural Institute (Birouli, Samastipur). LNMU has established a Directorate of Distance Education that became fully functional in 2005. LNMU admissions are primarily entrance-based. Aspirants are required to appear for relevant entrance exams to be considered for admission at LNMU. Students can also appear for the LNMU entrance exam in addition to providing scores obtained in various national and state-level entrance tests.

paragraphs: LMNU course admissionis a multi-step process that requires the aspirants to appear for relevant entrance exams conducted either by the university or national-level entrance exams. The university also accepts students on a merit basis for some of its courses. Students seeking admission to any of the programmes offered at the university can follow the steps to complete theLalit Narayan Mithila Universityadmission process:
1. LNMU Application Process

Application forms are available online on the official website of the university. Candidates must fill the form before the last date to apply for admission. As admissions to a lot of courses are entrance-based, candidates must first appear for the relevant entrance exam before applying for the course.
2. Selection

LNMU shortlists students on the basis of the score secured by them in either the last qualifying exam or the entrance test. The university conducts its own Common Entrance Test and also accepts various national and state-level entrance tests, such as MAT/ CMAT, etc. Shortlisted students' names will be posted on the official website ofLalit Narayan Mithila University. Students can check out the merit list once it is posted online.
3. Fee Payment and Document Verification

Selected candidates will then be required to pay the admission confirmation fees to secure their admission atLNMU. Alternatively, forMBA admission at LNMU,students can also obtain the prescribed form and pay INR 1,000 through Cash/DD/ in favour of Director Management Courses, Department of Commerce & Business Administration, LNMU, accompanied by a bank draft of INR 1,050.

TheLNMU feevaries depending on the course and specialisation selected by the student. It includes variou components such as admission fees, tuition fees, application fees, etc. Some of these components are charged only once at the time of admission, while the remaining are to be paid either semesterly or yearly. faqs.question: How can I get admission to LNMU for BTech programme?
faqs.answer: LNMU BTech admission is offered on the basis of candidate’s performance in the Combined Admission Test, conducted by the University. Shortlisted candidates are further called for counselling-cum-seat allotment round.

faqs.answer: Lalit Narayan Mithila University conductsPATfor accepting admission to the programme in September, every year. faqs.question: How much does BSc at LNMU cost?
faqs.answer: Lalit Narayan Mithila University (LNMU) is famous for providing affordable education. It offers a full-time BSc courses in several specialisations. The LNMU BSc tuition fee ranges between INR 30 and INR 4,800 for various specialisations. In addition to the tuition fees, the LNMU BSc fee structure includes one-time fees ranging from INR 75 to INR 180. It must be noted that the mentioned fee has been sourced from the official website of the university/sanctioning body. It is still subject to change and hence, is indicative.

faqs.question: What is the fee for B.Ed at Lalit Narayan Mithila University?
faqs.answer: Lalit Narayan Mithila University offers two-year B.Ed courses to students. After completing the B.Ed course, the students are placed in top companies. To check the fee, visit the official website of Lalit Narayan Mithila University. It is a state university so the fee will not be very high but if the students feel the fee is high, they can apply online for the scholarships offered by the university. The seats for the scholarships are limited so apply early. Check the Other B.Ed Colleges in Bihar

faqs.question: What is the cost of MBA in LNMU?
faqs.answer: At the postgraduate level, LNMU offers MBA programme to students. The total tuition fees for MBA ranges between INR 50,000 to INR 1.1 lakh. The accepted entrance exams are MAT, CMAT, and others.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2023Offered by Chandradhari Mithila College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 13 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: A budget friendly BCA college that focuses more on academics than placements.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure of CM College was basic but functional. Classrooms were spacious and well ventilated. Though some needed modernization. Computer labs were available with sufficient systems for BCA students. Advanced software and the latest hardware needed improvement.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by M.K.S. CollegeReviewed on 9 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: I am absolutely satisfied with this college during my session 2022–2025.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi and labs are not available at my college, but classrooms are available. There are many classrooms in my college. All classrooms are open for students. The library is also available in my college for reading books and self-study.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Ram Krishna College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 28 Nov 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Overall experience of my college life.
reviews.review_text: Lack of Wi-Fi, not good infrastructure, the whole college old buildings need renovation, lack of smart class, teachers have to teach with traditional methods of writing on the board, etc. New Education Policy Books are not available in the library for many courses like ours. The college has a mess and canteen facility, but lacks medical facilities and many sports to play on campus.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2024Offered by A.P.S.M. CollegeReviewed on 24 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with the college as per the fees.
reviews.review_text: Talking about their infrastructure overall, it was good that they provided a good classroom to their students, but they did not provide any type of canteen or medical facilities. But they provide sports games, and no library, Wi-Fi or labs are available for the students.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by A.P.S.M. CollegeReviewed on 23 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: APSM College, Barauni is an affiliated unit of Lalit Narayan Mithila University, Darbhanga.
reviews.review_text: It is a nice college. The college emphasizes activities like sports, NCC, and NSS for the all-round development of the students. The campus is equipped with Wi-Fi. It has a library, language laboratory, and various counselling cell (career counselling, grievance, women empowerment, etc). It is located in Barauni, Begusarai district, Bihar. But there is no hostel facility.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Bali Ram Bhagat College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 14 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Bali Ram Bhagat College, Lalit Narayan Mithila University .
reviews.review_text: The college has very basic infrastructure for Arts courses. Classrooms are simple, and the library is available but not very resourceful for English Honours students. Wi-Fi and digital facilities are not dependable, and computers are outdated. Hostel facilities are average, with food in the mess being very simple and repetitive, medical help is available in case of emergencies but is not very advanced. Sports and extracurricular activities are limited, and students usually manage with what is pr...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Ram Krishna College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 13 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: My college is best for students who are poor.
reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure available for my course and department are nice. The Wi-Fi service, labs, classrooms, and library are all great. The quality of the facilities in my hostel is nice, as is the quality of food in the mess and canteen. The medical facilities, sports, and games are also nice.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by RCSS CollegeReviewed on 13 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Overall it is good for the money.
reviews.review_text: They provide average infrastructure like classrooms, a library, and a lab, but there is no sign of a canteen or other related items. Some stalls are near the college, but their food is not too good. The classrooms are also good, and they provide sports and games as well.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2022Offered by A.P.S.M. CollegeReviewed on 3 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied with this college.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure was good and students could enjoy it well. Their classrooms were a little bit short but overall it's good. Wi-Fi and library were also good. There was no food. Sports and games were available for the students, and overall it's good.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by A.P.S.M. CollegeReviewed on 3 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied because of their fees and faculty members.
reviews.review_text: Their infrastructure is not too good, not too bad. It is an average college, and I think students can enjoy this college infrastructure very well because their buildings, their classes are also good, and now they provide many facilities that are very helpful for their students.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Ram Krishna College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 31 Aug 2025)
reviews.review_heading: The best thing about my course is that it makes students better in their jobs.
reviews.review_text: The facilities and ingredients available for my course/department are the best compared to other colleges in terms of facilities and infrastructure. The quality of the facilities in my hostel is very nice, as is the quality of food available in the mess and canteen. Medical facilities, sports, and games are also available in nice quality.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Ram Krishna College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 19 Aug 2025)
reviews.review_heading: About my college and information.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is not as expected but it's good for career focused students. Hostel facility is not good, especially for girls. Hostel is so old, and the management never made an effort to clean it. There are rooms for students with canteen facilities. Medical facilities are also available for students. The biggest thing is that there is a very big playground for students who want to play. Many events like cricket matches are being organised on campus every year.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2023Offered by Chandradhari Mithila College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 23 Jun 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Overall, college is normal.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi was provided in hostel. But labs in commerce department were not available. Every batch had separate classroom. Library for students was also available. In hostel, only living facilities were provided. Food was not provided. Mess or canteen was not available in hostel.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by A.P.S.M. CollegeReviewed on 16 Jun 2025)
reviews.review_heading: College is good, anyone can apply for it. Teachers and infrastructure are also nice.
reviews.review_text: I am a regular student and have not taken the hostel. But the hostel facilities are good. Infrastructure of the college is nice. The environment is also good. Classroom and library need a little specification. Wi-Fi service is so nice. The speed is good, and 24-hour Wi-Fi is available on campus.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2024Offered by kunwar Singh College, Lalit Narayan Mithila UniversityReviewed on 14 Jun 2025)
reviews.review_heading: College infrastructure is good, and faculty are well behaved.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure of the college is good and looks better, but the college hostel is not available for students. I don’t think so because I have never seen any kind of hostel in the college, so I can’t say anything about the hostel, but the infrastructure is good.

faqs.answer: A:    No, you cannot become a lawyer without a Law degree. You mandatorily need to obtain degrees such as BA/BCom/BSc/BBA LLB or LLB in order to be a lawyer in India. To become a lawyer, one must first complete three-year or five-year LLB from a recognised university and possess degree certificate. Then, they must enrol themselves in local Bar Council of the concerned state and enrolled as an advocate. Within two years of the enrolment, they must appear and clear AIBE in order to be eligible to practise anywhere in India.

faqs.question: Can I become a lawyer after completing my LLB?
faqs.answer: No, After completing LLB you become a lawyer, however, you are still not an advocate and hence cannot practice law.To become an advocate and to practice law as a profession, you mandatorily need to clear theAll India Bar Examination (AIBE). After qualifying AIBE, one can practice law as a full fledged profession.

faqs.question: Can I start a law practice without a Law degree or become a lawyer?
faqs.answer: No, you cannot become a lawyer without a Law degree.You mandatorily need to obtain degrees such as BA/BCom/BSc/BBA LLB or LLB in order to be a lawyer in India. To become a lawyer, one must first complete three-year or five-year LLB from a recognised university and possess degree certificate. Then, they must enrol themselves in local Bar Council of the concerned state and enrolled as an advocate. Within two years of the enrolment, they must appear and clear AIBE in order to be eligible to practise anywhere in India.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Jodhpur National University.
reviews.review_text: There are six semesters of taught courses, including a summer internship. The total credit for the programmes is 140, which is equivalent to 26 courses. Each credit represents 1 hour of classroom teaching per week and 15 hours in a semester. Hostel accommodation is provided separately for boys and girls. The infrastructure of the hostels is good, and the rooms are double-sharing and well-furnished. Maintenance services are provided, and the mess food, as well as Wi-Fi, is also good.

reviews.review_heading: It is the best college in all of India.
reviews.review_text: The transport facilities, hostel facilities, food, and many more amenities can't be explained enough; this is the best college according to my experience. It also has a big playground. I suggest that everyone should take admission in this college.

reviews.review_heading: I am dissatisfied with 2015 campus placements.
reviews.review_text: Facilities like Wi-Fi, labs and library were in good condition. Food in a mess used to be unhygienic and lacked good quality. No inter-college sports competitions were conducted. In the library, there were only a few books. No medical and sports facilities were provided in the college.

reviews.review_heading: Dissatisfied because of no placement.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure was good and well built but, due to lack of students it was hard to maintain the property. Labs had proper equipments. No Wi-Fi available for students it was only accessible at college computers. Library was good having the necessary books.

reviews.review_heading: I am very disappointed with Jodhpur National University.
reviews.review_text: The campus is huge spreading over 1000 acres. Classrooms were not well-designed, and the facilities were not good as the green boards and seats were broken. No Wi-Fi facility is available on the campus. Labs are good, but the lab operators are not available properly. The library has a less number of books. There is no sports room nor games. Overall, I am not happy with the college.

reviews.review_heading: You can join the college, but it's not worth it..
reviews.review_text: As far as the infrastructure is concerned, we have all the facilities. The Wi-Fi facility was available but was paid. We had all the sports facilities like badminton, cricket, table tennis etc. Rooms provided to us were good in terms of quality, and they were spacious. Food served in the canteen was good.

reviews.review_heading: This is the West Rajasthan deemed private university.
reviews.review_text: Jodhpur National University infrastructure is average. There is no proper branch building. There are three to four branches in one building. In the laboratory, all the equipment is not available. This university is located outside the city, and the transportation system is not good. The hostel management is poor, and they provide low quality food.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with the freedom and over friendly faculty members.
reviews.review_text: Here, Wi-Fi labs, classrooms, library, medical hospital, restrooms, football ground, cricket ground, badminton court, basketball court, garden, cafeteria, laundry, hostel facilities, vegetarian mess, common room, a Wi-Fi enabled hostel and a good number of cadavers are present.

reviews.review_heading: Completely fed up with college placements. The only good thing is faculty, otherwise, a full waste
reviews.review_text: In our college, the infrastructure was average. The lab facility and the library are good. We are getting books for a long duration. Talking about the canteen and the hostel, they are the same as all colleges. The canteen food was not so hygienic and costly too. Hostel rooms are average as they are very old. Labs and classrooms are average. In the lab, limited pieces of equipment were there so we need to share. In the computer lab, we need to share the computer. Classrooms were big but fully unf...

reviews.review_heading: Bad placements, but the infrastructure is good and you have to do everything on your own.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure was huge but not properly managed. The college has a canteen with really good food. There are separate boys and girls hostel. The mess food is really good, especially the breakfast. You can eat in the mess secretly even if you are not staying in the hostel. It has a good big hospital with all the modern facilities and also has a medical shop inside the campus. The college transportation facilities are very good and they have many buses. You can find shops outside the campus al...

reviews.review_heading: It is an average university with average faculty, where the management wasn't up to the mark at all.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure was huge but not well-maintained. The washrooms were not clean and neither were they cleaned on a regular basis. The classrooms were not comfortable enough for the students as well. They had grounds huge enough to play any kind of sport but they were also not maintained. The canteen was small and the food quality was also not good.

reviews.review_heading: No placement was there.I think placement is the most important thing which matters.
reviews.review_text: No sports faculty and coach available. The infrastructure is typical Rajasthani building surrounded by sand. The inside infrastructure is more or less ok. In the summers, it creates problems for students to sit inside the classroom because there is no AC, no cooler. Only fan is there.

reviews.review_heading: If you are still in this college and you are reading this then follow the NPTEL videos.
reviews.review_text:
The Wi-Fi is good, but you have to pay Rs 200 or more per month, and most of the sites are blocked.

The classrooms are without any facility like no projector, light and fan problem always, with no laptop charging point.
The medical facilities are like you have to pay every time just like a private clinic.
For sports, there is only 1 badminton court but no net facility.

reviews_list.review_title: Value for money with great knowledge but lacks proper campus.

review_content.Placements: After graduation, anyone can pursue multiple career options like medical officer by exams, post graduation, private clinics, jobs, etc., which is to be chosen independent of college. review_content.Infrastructure: No public Wi-Fi. Physiology and anatomy labs are available but averagely maintained. No canteen in college and no sports are played in general by students. reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine & Surgery (B.A.M.S.) - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: It has life maintaining practical course with great potential and raising sports.

review_content.Placements: B.A.M.S. is an independent degree that does not require any placements in any particular hospital. One can open their clinic independently or give an exam to medical officer of any state government which is a very stable job. So the placement is almost 100% for everyone. Some people also prepare for administrative services after doing B.A.M.S. so that is also feasible option.
review_content.Infrastructure: This college building is an old but undergoing renovation continuously. Our college has physiology lab, anatomy lab, dravya guna garden, and RSBK department lab. Boys' hostel is not in good shape when I first entered. But now it has been provided good funds and better management by students. It is a very good option if we compare it to any other private hostel or anything. Food is provided by contact services.

reviews.review_heading: Value for money with great knowledge but lacks proper campus.
reviews.review_text: No public Wi-Fi. Physiology and anatomy labs are available but averagely maintained. No canteen in college and no sports are played in general by students. Not enough space for playing. The quality of mess food available is moderate as seen in private hostels. Not living in a government hostel.

reviews.review_heading: It has life maintaining practical course with great potential and raising sports.
reviews.review_text: This college building is an old but undergoing renovation continuously. Our college has physiology lab, anatomy lab, dravya guna garden, and RSBK department lab. It has a well-stocked library. But there is no Wi-Fi or playground. Sports are not much in our college but students go to play individually with their own money. Boys' hostel is not in good shape when I first entered. But now it has been provided good funds and better management by students. It is a very good option if we compare it to ...
